items.text: Heads up, everyone! The Tech Lab in Bryant Hall is offering totally free printing all week, through this Friday, May 8. After that, the usual $0.10 per page charge returns. So grab your lab reports and class slides now while it lasts!

questions.stem: According to the post, when does the free printing offer end?
questions.explanation: The post says free printing runs through Friday, May 8, which is when the offer ends.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about printing at the Tech Lab after the offer ends?

options.A: Students will be charged for each page
options.B: Printing will no longer be allowed there
options.C: Students will need a special access code

questions.explanation: The post notes the usual ten-cents-per-page charge returns afterward, implying students must pay per page once the offer ends.

items.text: Hi Daniel, your resume review with Career Services is confirmed for Thursday, April 16, at 3:00 PM in Room 210. Please bring two printed copies of your current resume. If you can't make it, reply to reschedule. Best, Ms. Reyes.

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Daniel bring to the appointment?

options.A: Two printed copies of his resume
options.B: A list of preferred job openings
options.D: A signed copy of his transcript

questions.explanation: The email asks Daniel to bring two printed copies of his current resume.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Daniel's options if he is unavailable?

options.A: He must cancel the service entirely
options.B: He should visit a different office
options.C: He will lose his appointment slot
options.D: He can arrange a different time

questions.explanation: The email invites him to reply to reschedule, implying he can arrange a different time if unavailable.

items.text: Notice to all Lincoln Hall residents: Fire alarm testing will be conducted on Tuesday, March 10, from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM. Alarms will sound intermittently during this period. Residents are advised that no evacuation is required while testing is underway.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the fire alarm testing take place?

options.A: On Monday, March 9, in the evening
options.B: On Tuesday, March 10, in the morning
options.D: On Thursday, March 12, in the afternoon

questions.explanation: The notice states testing will occur on Tuesday, March 10, from 9:00 to 11:00 AM, which falls in the morning.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what are residents told to do during the testing?

options.A: To gather outside the main entrance
options.B: To report any alarm to the front desk
options.D: To shut off the alarms in their rooms

questions.explanation: The notice advises that no evacuation is required, meaning residents should remain inside during the testing.

items.text: The elevator in Carver Library will be out of service from June 22 to June 24 for repairs. Visitors needing upper-floor materials may request retrieval at the front desk for a $2 handling fee. The east stairwell remains open throughout the closure.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the cost of the retrieval service?

options.A: A refundable deposit of five dollars
options.B: A weekly charge of three dollars
options.C: A one-time fine of four dollars
options.D: A handling fee of two dollars

questions.explanation: The notice states a two-dollar handling fee applies for requesting retrieval at the front desk.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about reaching the upper floors during the closure?

options.A: The building will be fully closed
options.C: The stairs can still be used

questions.explanation: Since the east stairwell remains open throughout, visitors can still reach upper floors using the stairs.
